records.ety: [OE. deyen, dien, of Scand. origin; cf. Icel. deyja; akin to Dan. döe, Sw. dö, Goth. diwan (cf. Goth. afd<?/jan to harass), OFries. d<?/ia to kill, OS. doian to die, OHG. touwen, OSlav. daviti to choke, Lith. dovyti to torment. Cf. Dead, Death.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Died (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Dying.]

defs: To pass from an animate to a lifeless state; to cease to live; to suffer a total and irreparable loss of action of the vital functions; to become dead; to expire; to perish; -- said of animals and vegetables; often with of, by, with, from, and rarely for, before the cause or occasion of death; as, to die of disease or hardships; to die by fire or the sword; to die with horror at the thought.
quotes: To die by the roadside of grief and hunger.
She will die from want of care.

defs: To suffer death; to lose life.
quotes: In due time Christ died for the ungodly.

defs: To perish in any manner; to cease; to become lost or extinct; to be extinguished.
quotes: Letting the secret die within his own breast.

defs: To sink; to faint; to pine; to languish, with weakness, discouragement, love, etc.
quotes: His heart died within, and he became as a stone.
The young men acknowledged, in love letters, that they died for Rebecca.

defs: To become indifferent; to cease to be subject; as, to die to pleasure or to sin.
To recede and grow fainter; to become imperceptible; to vanish; -- often with out or away.
quotes: Blemishes may die away and disappear amidst the brightness.

defs: To disappear gradually in another surface, as where moldings are lost in a sloped or curved face.
To become vapid, flat, or spiritless, as liquor.
quotes: “There is one certain way,” replied the Prince [William of Orange] “ by which I can be sure never to see my country's ruin, -- I will die in the last ditch.”

records.ety: [OE. dee, die, F. d, fr. L. datus given, thrown, p. p. of dare to give, throw. See Date a point of time.]

defs: A small cube, marked on its faces with spots from one to six, and used in playing games by being shaken in a box and thrown from it. See Dice.
Any small cubical or square body.
quotes: Words . . . pasted upon little flat tablets or dies.

defs: That which is, or might be, determined, by a throw of the die; hazard; chance.
quotes: Such is the die of war.

defs: That part of a pedestal included between base and cornice; the dado.

A metal or plate (often one of a pair) so cut or shaped as to give a certain desired form to, or impress any desired device on, an object or surface, by pressure or by a blow; used in forging metals, coining, striking up sheet metal, etc.

A perforated block, commonly of hardened steel used in connection with a punch, for punching holes, as through plates, or blanks from plates, or for forming cups or capsules, as from sheet metal, by drawing.
A hollow internally threaded screw-cutting tool, made in one piece or composed of several parts, for forming screw threads on bolts, etc.; one of the separate parts which make up such a tool.
